context: Tests whether the agent implements consent recording with all required metadata fields, designs consent logs as append-only, captures consent server-side, notifies the ESP on GDPR erasure, suppresses customers from ad platforms on CCPA opt-out, and separates marketing consent from transactional email handling.

checklist.name: IP address captured
checklist.description: recordConsent captures and stores the customer's IP address in the consent record

checklist.name: User agent captured
checklist.description: recordConsent captures and stores the request's user agent string in the consent record

checklist.name: Policy version captured
checklist.description: recordConsent captures and stores the privacy policy version in effect at the time of consent

checklist.name: Consent method captured
checklist.description: recordConsent captures the method by which consent was given (e.g. 'checkbox', 'preference-center', 'double-opt-in')

checklist.name: Append-only consent log
checklist.description: consent-manager.ts uses an insert/create operation (never update/patch/upsert) for storing consent records, and consent-design.md describes the logs as append-only

checklist.name: No consent record deletion
checklist.description: consent-manager.ts does NOT call any delete/destroy/remove operation on consent log records

checklist.name: ESP notified on erasure
checklist.description: handleErasureRequest calls a method on the email service client to delete or suppress the customer's profile (e.g. emailServiceClient.deleteProfile)

checklist.name: CCPA suppresses Google Customer Match
checklist.description: handleCcpaOptOut calls a method to suppress the customer from Google Customer Match (e.g. adPlatformClient.suppressFromCustomerMatch)

checklist.name: CCPA suppresses Meta Custom Audiences
checklist.description: handleCcpaOptOut calls a method to suppress the customer from Meta/Facebook Custom Audiences (e.g. adPlatformClient.suppressFromCustomAudiences)

checklist.name: Marketing vs transactional separation
checklist.description: consent-design.md explicitly states that transactional emails (order confirmation, shipping) do not require marketing consent, and the code or documentation reflects this separation

checklist.name: Server-side capture documented
checklist.description: consent-design.md states that consent is captured server-side (at the API layer), not purely client-side

checklist.name: Consent type enum/union
checklist.description: recordConsent or ConsentRecord uses a typed consent type (e.g. 'marketing-email' | 'marketing-sms' | 'data-processing') rather than a plain untyped string
